title: LEXON-Bench: Executable AI Regulatory Obligation Reasoning — Companion Benchmark Repository
description: LEXON-Bench is the companion benchmark repository for the manuscript 'LEXON: Executable AI Regulatory Obligation Reasoning with Typed Knowledge Graphs and Stratified Datalog' (MDPI Information). It provides a deterministic synthetic benchmark (25 clauses, 30 AI system profiles, 750 clause-profile instances) for evaluating executable obligation activation, evidence-gap detection, cross-clause conflict detection, and evidence-remediation candidate generation under explicit closed-world assumptions and stratified-Datalog inference. Includes benchmark data, gold labels, Datalog rule specifications, Python baseline implementations (static checklist, ontology-only, flat-rule engine), evaluation scripts, bootstrap CI computation, paper-ready tables, and a single-command reproduction pipeline. All random seeds are fixed (seed=42); results are deterministic and fully reproducible.
notes: Companion repository for the LEXON manuscript submitted to MDPI Information. Results: LEXON achieves corpus-level F1=1.000 for T1 (obligation activation) and T2 (evidence-gap detection) on the 150-instance held-out test set; T3 (conflict detection) F1=0.957 (TP=11, FP=1, FN=0). All outputs are reproducible via 'make reproduce' with seed=42. DUAL LICENSE: Source code is released under the MIT License (see LICENSE). Synthetic benchmark data (data/ directory) are released under CC-BY-4.0 (see LICENSE-DATA).
